source_pr.title: executor: fix data race in the slowQueryRuntimeStats
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: Weizhen Wang <wangweizhen@pingcap.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

-->

Issue Number: close #39631

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note

```

symptoms: Race detector reports a data race involving `slowQueryRuntimeStats` fields (e.g., `readFile`) being updated while runtime stats are being cloned/registered.
failure_signature: DATA RACE in executor/slow_query.go: concurrent mutation/read of `slowQueryRuntimeStats` during slow query parsing / runtime stats collection.
root_cause_explanation: `slowQueryRuntimeStats` is updated while parsing slow logs (e.g., accumulating `readFile` duration). Previously, runtime stats could be registered/cloned while the retriever was still running, leading to concurrent read/write on the same stats object (triggering the race detector). The fix defers `RuntimeStatsColl.RegisterStats(...)` until after the retriever is closed, ensuring the stats are no longer being mutated when they are registered/merged/cloned.
fix_pattern: Defer runtime stats registration until after the slow-query retriever is closed to avoid concurrent mutation/read of `slowQueryRuntimeStats`.

evidence: evidence: executor: fix data race in the slowQueryRuntimeStats
